title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the force exerted by the glove on the ball, we need to consider the change in momentum of the ball and the time it takes to stop.

title: Calculate the Velocity Before Impact
text: Use the formula for the final velocity of an object in free fall: $$v=\sqrt{2g h}$$, where g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s²) and $$h$$ is the height $$(60.0$$ m).

title: Determine the Change in Momentum
text: The change in momentum (Δp) is given by the formula: $$Δp=m v$$, where $$m$$ is the mass of the ball (145 g converted to kg) and v is the velocity just before impact.

title: Calculate the Force
text: The force exerted by the glove can be calculated using the formula: $$F=\frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t}$$, where Δt is the time taken to stop the ball $$(0.0100$$ s).

title: Final Calculation
text: Using the values calculated, find the force exerted by the glove on the ball.
